steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between the two groups. A valid answer must name both the Choctaw Code Talkers and the Navajo Code Talkers and show something they had in common — a statement about only one group, or a contrast between them, fails the goal.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "US soldiers who were members of the Choctaw Nation in Oklahoma used their native language to relay coded information.": Describes only the Choctaw Code Talkers. The Navajo Code Talkers are not mentioned, so no similarity is drawn. ✗
  • "In World War II, one group of Navajo (Diné) soldiers was known as the Navajo Code Talkers.": Introduces only the Navajo Code Talkers. The Choctaw Code Talkers are not mentioned, so no similarity is drawn. ✗
  • "Both the Choctaw Code Talkers and the Navajo Code Talkers transmitted coded military messages in the soldiers' native languages.": Names both groups and states a shared trait — transmitting coded messages in their native languages. This directly establishes a similarity. ✓
  • "The Choctaw Code Talkers, not the Navajo Code Talkers, served in World War I.": Names both groups but emphasizes a difference (different wars), not a similarity. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
